title: Cannot retrieve pod logs without specifying container name using the -c flag
body:
### Issue Description

Unable to get logs of pod without passing container name using -c flag

❯ podman pod logs  6c2b95d137066b0c5852423291f713200e48afcc8cc8d02bb39935afcb964f9d 
Error: -c or --container cannot be empty: invalid argument

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Podman run a pod by `podman play kube file.yaml`
2. Try to get the logs of all container logs within the pod.
3. Podman throws error, `Error: -c or --container cannot be empty: invalid argument`


### Describe the results you received

`Error: -c or --container cannot be empty: invalid argument`

### Describe the results you expected

Should be able to get the logs of all containers in the pod.

comments.body: @mersalin May I know if you know a workaround for this issue? I face the same issue
